metadata.note: Iteration 1 used post-hoc grading of prior outputs. This iteration runs fresh independent subagents per the updated context-eval methodology.

per_eval_breakdown.note: A1 (Step 0) was weak in with-harness run — mentioned but not led with

diagnosis.reasoning: Mean benefit of +0.35 across 3 fresh subagent-pair evals. Consistent improvement in all scenarios. With-harness pass rate 0.94 vs baseline 0.59. The 0.06 miss (eval 1, A1) suggests Step 0 could be more prominent in the skill.
diagnosis.comparison_with_post_hoc: Post-hoc analysis (iteration 1) reported +0.45 and 1.00 with-harness. Fresh run shows +0.35 and 0.94. Post-hoc was inflated by ~10pp, confirming that post-hoc grading is systematically more generous than fresh subagent evaluation.
highest_impact_areas: Step 0 — verify actual context (discriminating in 2/3 evals)
Revert-first discipline for regressions (discriminating in 1/1 eval)
non_discriminating_assertions: Basic category identification — baseline already does this well
Quick fix suggestions — baseline produces decent ad-hoc fixes
recommendations: Step 0 was weak in eval 1 — consider making it more structurally prominent (before the triage table, not just before Step 1)
Compound failure detection is the highest-value differentiator — could add more examples
Post-hoc evaluation inflates results by ~10pp — always use fresh subagent pairs for methodology harnesses
